classification: real-driving-sequence reconstruction-based counterfactual closed-loop neural simulator
log_driven_definition: A simulator that constructs an interactive environment from real road-driving capture sequences and generates counterfactual closed-loop evolution; exact log replay is not required.

levels.name: source_availability_and_provenance
evidence: released scene, vehicle, and scenario assets
levels.main_gap: The complete reconstruction provenance is not yet packaged as an independently downloadable evidence bundle.

levels.name: closed_loop_evidence_completeness
evidence: synchronized RGB, semantic, and depth observations
levels.main_gap: The current deterministic planner is a loop enabler rather than a sensor-input AD agent.

levels.name: segment_level_credibility_judgment
evidence: identical ego states and actions across conditions
internal geometry and metric response to actor placement
levels.main_gap: Visible rendering domain gap, no matched real-log reference comparison, and insufficient intervention coverage.

levels.name: cross_segment_simulator_credibility
evidence: decision rules and a compact audit record format exist
levels.main_gap: Too few independently grounded segments across scenes and event types to define an aggregate credibility metric.

current_claim: HUGSIM is a suitable first case study for developing the four-level method, but current experiments do not establish sensor-level E2E evaluation credibility or global simulator credibility.
